channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: LLM-Augmented Knowledge Base Construction For Root Cause Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.06171v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Communications networks now form the backbone of our digital world, with fast and reliable connectivity. However, even with appropriate redundancy and failover mechanisms, it is difficult to guarantee "five 9s" (99.999 %) reliability, requiring rapid ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, knowledge base, RAG, analysis, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Stepwise Informativeness Assumption: Why are Entropy Dynamics and Reasoning Correlated in LLMs?
item.description: arXiv:2604.06192v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work uses entropy-based signals at multiple representation levels to study reasoning in large language models, but the field remains largely empirical. A central unresolved puzzle is why internal entropy dynamics, defined under the predictive d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, arxiv, study, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Depression Detection at the Point of Care: Automated Analysis of Linguistic Signals from Routine Primary Care Encounters
item.description: arXiv:2604.06193v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Depression is underdiagnosed in primary care, yet timely identification remains critical. Recorded clinical encounters, increasingly common with digital scribing technologies, present an opportunity to detect depression from naturalistic dialogue. We ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, analysis, GPT, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Emergent decentralized regulation in a purely synthetic society
item.description: arXiv:2604.06199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As autonomous AI agents increasingly inhabit online environments and extensively interact, a key question is whether synthetic collectives exhibit self-regulated social dynamics with neither human intervention nor centralized design. We study OpenClaw...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, study, model, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Facts: Benchmarking Distributional Reading Comprehension in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.06201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While most reading comprehension benchmarks for LLMs focus on factual information that can be answered by localizing specific textual evidence, many real-world tasks require understanding distributional information, such as population-level trends and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, LLM, arxiv, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cross-Lingual Transfer and Parameter-Efficient Adaptation in the Turkic Language Family: A Theoretical Framework for Low-Resource Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.06202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have transformed natural language processing, yet their capabilities remain uneven across languages. Most multilingual models are trained primarily on high-resource languages, leaving many languages with large speaker popu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, arxiv, study, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ProofSketcher: Hybrid LLM + Lightweight Proof Checker for Reliable Math/Logic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.06401v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The large language models (LLMs) might produce a persuasive argument within mathematical and logical fields, although such argument often includes some minor missteps, including the entire omission of side conditions, invalid inference patterns, or ap...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, paper, LLM, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Benchmark of Classical and Deep Learning Models for Agricultural Commodity Price Forecasting on A Novel Bangladeshi Market Price Dataset
item.description: arXiv:2604.06227v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate short-term forecasting of agricultural commodity prices is critical for food security planning and smallholder income stabilisation in developing economies, yet machine-learning-ready datasets for this purpose remain scarce in South Asia. Thi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, transformer, ICL, paper, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FLeX: Fourier-based Low-rank EXpansion for multilingual transfer
item.description: arXiv:2604.06253v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-lingual code generation is critical in enterprise environments where multiple programming languages coexist. However, fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) individually for each language is computationally prohibitive. This paper investigates...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, arxiv, fine-tuning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TalkLoRA: Communication-Aware Mixture of Low-Rank Adaptation for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.06291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) enables parameter-efficient fine-tuning of Large Language Models (LLMs), and recent Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) extensions further enhance flexibility by dynamically combining multiple LoRA experts. However, existing MoE-augmen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, paper, arxiv, augmented, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SymptomWise: A Deterministic Reasoning Layer for Reliable and Efficient AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.06375v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-driven symptom analysis systems face persistent challenges in reliability, interpretability, and hallucination. End-to-end generative approaches often lack traceability and may produce unsupported or inconsistent diagnostic outputs in safety-critic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, framework, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On Emotion-Sensitive Decision Making of Small Language Model Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.06562v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Small language models (SLM) are increasingly used as interactive decision-making agents, yet most decision-oriented evaluations ignore emotion as a causal factor influencing behavior. We study emotion-sensitive decision making by combining representat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, template, arxiv, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: High-Precision Estimation of the State-Space Complexity of Shogi via the Monte Carlo Method
item.description: arXiv:2604.06189v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Determining the state-space complexity of the game of Shogi (Japanese Chess) has been a challenging problem, with previous combinatorial estimates leaving a gap of five orders of magnitude ($10^{64}$ to $10^{69}$). This large gap arises from the diffi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.06256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training dynamics during grokking concentrate along a small number of dominant update directions -- the spectral edge -- which reliably distinguishes grokking from non-grokking regimes. We show that standard mechanistic interpretability tools (head at...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: $S^3$: Stratified Scaling Search for Test-Time in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.06260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Test-time scaling investigates whether a fixed diffusion language model (DLM) can generate better outputs when given more inference compute, without additional training. However, naive best-of-$K$ sampling is fundamentally limited because it repeatedl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, reasoning, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Asymptotic-Preserving Neural Networks for Viscoelastic Parameter Identification in Multiscale Blood Flow Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2604.06287v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mathematical models and numerical simulations offer a non-invasive way to explore cardiovascular phenomena, providing access to quantities that cannot be measured directly. In this study, we start with a one-dimensional multiscale blood flow model tha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
